display.purpose: Hero trip titles laid over the photograph as a title card.

label.purpose: Sentence-case eyebrows, field labels, meta keys. Never a tracked-uppercase block.

shadows.name: print-frame
shadows.purpose: Hairline frame around photographs, gallery-print style. The only 'elevation' on images.

motion.name: ease-cinematic
motion.purpose: House ease-out for reveals, hover, and the hero cross-fade.

motion.name: hero-crossfade
motion.purpose: Trip image swap behind the gallery index.

motion.name: hero-push
motion.purpose: Slow 1.05x scale push on the active hero image.

motion.name: reveal
motion.purpose: Scroll-in reveal. Visible by default; JS-gated entrance with a transition-free safety net.

components.name: Gallery Index Title
components.description: Signature hero list item: bold grotesque trip title, obsession word in terracotta, tabular date.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Solid terracotta pill for primary actions (Request to book, Join the waitlist).

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Outlined pill for the hero nav and secondary actions on dark grounds.

components.name: Interest Chip
components.description: Multi-select interest pill on the dark form panel.

components.name: Underline Field
components.description: Boxless field on the dark form panel: off-white text over a bottom rule, label above.

narrative.overview: freaks is a near-black room hung with cinematic prints. Photography is the light source: full-bleed, grayscale-leaning frames under a heavy scrim, with the trip names set as bold type laid over the image like a title card. The rest of the page is a darkened gallery, charcoal walls, off-white text, hairline-framed photographs, and a single warm light that is terracotta. Terracotta marks the obsession word in every trip name, every link, every primary action, and the one terracotta room (the waitlist). Hierarchy comes from one grotesque typeface pushed across weights, not from decoration; numbers are tabular and exact; surfaces are flat.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black gallery ground; photography is the light
One warm light: terracotta as structural voice, not accent dust
A single grotesque (Bricolage Grotesque) across weights, no serif, no mono
Photographs framed like prints (hairline inset); flat surfaces, no shadows
The obsession word is terracotta + heavy weight; the place is off-white
Specificity as luxury: real names, real times, tabular benchmarks

rules.name: The One Light Rule
rules.body: Terracotta is the only chroma. It may appear generously, marking obsessions, links, actions, and the one terracotta room, but a second accent hue is never introduced.

rules.name: The Two-Ground Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are either a near-black ground or the single terracotta room, no third surface, no gradient between. On the terracotta room the form sits on an inset near-black panel so inputs stay legible.

rules.name: The Obsession-in-Terracotta Rule
rules.body: In any trip title, the thing you're obsessed with is terracotta at weight 700 and the place is off-white. Terracotta + weight marks the obsession, replacing the old serif-italic convention.

rules.name: The Tabular-Truth Rule
rules.body: All comparable numbers (benchmarks, distances, times, dates, prices) use tabular figures. The data is the proof of seriousness; it aligns.

rules.name: The Print-on-Black Rule
rules.body: Photographs are framed like gallery prints with a 1px inset hairline, never a drop shadow. For separation, change ground temperature or frame; do not lift.

dos: Do keep the page near-black with one terracotta room and one terracotta light.
Do mark the obsession word in terracotta + weight 700; keep the place off-white.
Do use a single typeface (Bricolage Grotesque) across weights.
Do frame photographs like prints with a hairline inset, and keep surfaces flat.
Do set every comparable number in tabular figures.
Do lead with specificity: name the expert, the hotel, the trail, the exact time.
Do inset the form on a dark panel when it sits on the terracotta surface.
donts: Don't reintroduce the editorial-typographic lane: no serif italics, no monospaced tracked labels, no hairline-rule eyebrows over every section, no warm cream paper.
Don't add a second accent hue or a second typeface.
Don't build a generic travel-operator layout: card grids, star ratings, 'book now' clutter, stock-smile photography.
Don't drift into hustle/retreat-bro wellness, corporate SaaS scaffolding, or mass-luxury influencer travel.
Don't use em dashes in copy; en dashes for numeric ranges only.
Don't put body or placeholder text directly on the mid-terracotta surface; use the dark panel or full off-white.
Don't drop shadows for depth; use temperature, framing, or the inset panel.
